Add Cloud Function sync proxy to bypass content blockers

a proxy between client and Firestore. This bypasses content blockers
that block direct Firestore SDK calls.

- Auto-restores via Cloud Function
- Works regardless of content blockers

Pro users can now sync across all devices without whitelisting Firebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(spaces): implement Spaces for multi-group data distribution

Add Space abstraction that controls how data is distributed across
multiple Raft groups. A Space with R replicas on an N-node cluster
creates N groups of R nodes each, with preferred leaders evenly
distributed via ring assignment. Data is hash-routed by primary key.

- Add sys.spaces system table (ID 7) with key encoding helpers
- Add CREATE/DROP/SHOW SPACE SQL support (lexer, parser, planner, executor)
- Add IN SPACE clause to CREATE TABLE for table-space binding
- Add space-aware routing (SpaceInfo, loadSpaces, routeToGroup) in raft_store
- Seed default space (replicas=ALL, single group) on bootstrap
- Add /api/spaces REST endpoint and Spaces tab in web dashboard

Fix button+text move: clicking text on a button now moves the whole group

its contained text as children.

Major Additions:

CRITIC AGENT (.claude/agents/critic.md)

SKILL SETS (src/skills/)
1. Evidence Verifier (evidence-verifier.js)
   - Validates all claims have file:line citations
   - Checks files exist in repository
   - Confirms timestamp format validity
   - Returns validation report with issues

2. Hallucination Detector (hallucination-detector.js)
   - Detects claims about non-existent fields/APIs
   - Validates function signatures match code
   - Checks data types match schema
   - Returns hallucination risk score (0.0-1.0)

3. Confidence Scorer (confidence-scorer.js)
   - Combines evidence quality + hallucination detection
   - Scoring formula: base + evidenceBonus - hallucinationPen - contradictionPen
   - Evidence weight: 25%, Hallucination: 35%, Contradiction: 20%
   - Returns final confidence (0.0-1.0)

MCP SUPPORT (src/mcp/)
- MCP Client (mcp-client.js): Initialize MCP transport, connect to server
- Context Providers (4 types):
  * RepoContextProvider: Repository structure, recent files, git history
  * LogContextProvider: Parse and provide log file contents
  * SchemaContextProvider: Database schema definitions
  * MetricsContextProvider: Performance metrics data
- Graceful degradation if MCP unavailable
- 5s timeout per request, context caching
- 100% test coverage for MCP

INTEGRATION

TESTING

  * mcp-context-providers.test.js - Context provider tests

CODE COVERAGE

- MCP: 100% coverage
- Orchestrator: 93.6% statement coverage
- Skills: Pending full integration tests

BACKWARDS COMPATIBILITY
- All 319 original tests still passing
- 48 new tests added
- Zero breaking changes
- Graceful degradation when MCP unavailable

QUALITY METRICS
- Tests: 367/367 passing (100%)
- Flake rate: 0% (all deterministic)
- Confidence scoring: Now targets 0.85+ (up from 0.70)
- Evidence verification: Automated validation added
- Hallucination detection: Risk scoring implemented

commit.message:
fix(companion): use separate HOME to avoid glibc claude binary

The shared PVC has a glibc-linked claude binary at /config/.local/bin/
installed by daedalus (Debian). Companion runs on Alpine (musl) and
picks this up via PATH resolution, causing "posix_getdents: symbol not
found" errors.

Fix by setting HOME=/home/companion (emptyDir) so the musl-compatible
npm claude at /app/node_modules/.bin/ is found instead. PVC mounted
at /workspace for project access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Perf: flat TT + incremental bitboards (+14.5%, 4.49M -> 5.14M NPS)

Two optimizations targeting the search engine hot path:

1. Flat transposition table (gtsa.hpp):
   Replace std::unordered_map with a fixed-size power-of-2 array.
   The old unordered_map allocated a heap node per TT insertion (~225K
   per search turn), causing ~7% of runtime in malloc_consolidate and
   related allocator functions. The flat table uses direct hash indexing
   (slot = hash & mask) with "always replace" collision policy — standard
   in chess engines. Zero heap allocations during search, single array
   access instead of hash bucket + linked list traversal.

   Table size: 2^18 = 256K entries (~7 MB, fits in L3 cache).
   Collision detection reuses the existing verification_key (64-bit hash
   from a different function than the index hash). Empty slots have
   verification_key == 0 as sentinel.

   Also removed virtual destructor from TTEntry (was never used
   polymorphically), saving 8 bytes per entry and eliminating vtable
   pointer overhead. Made TTEntry::to_stream const-correct.

2. Incremental player bitboards (wordescape.cpp):
   Maintain mPlayer1Bits and mPlayer2Bits (BitBoard, 3 x uint64_t each)
   as part of the game state, updated in setCellState alongside the
   existing grid/hash/goodness updates.

   Before: make_move's connectivity check scanned all 130 grid cells to
   build the enemy bitboard and identify home-edge seeds:
     for (y = 0..12) for (x = 0..9) if (grid[y][x] == enemy) ...

   After: directly use the maintained bitboard + precomputed edge mask:
     enemyBits = (enemy == P1) ? mPlayer1Bits : mPlayer2Bits;
     homeEdge  = enemyBits & edgeMask;    // single AND on 3 words

   Cost: 48 extra bytes per state copy (2 x 24 bytes), negligible vs
   the 1KB InlineBitset already being copied.

Benchmark (depth 4, 200 moves, 5s):
  Before: 4.49M NPS
  After:  5.14M NPS (overall), 5.25-5.31M steady-state
  Improvement: +14.5%
  Total: 12.2x from original baseline (421K)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(BL-034): dual temperament profiles — applyHoleResultDual

Each player has primary + secondary temperamentProfile. Secondary always
contributes: composureDelta = primaryDelta + Math.trunc(secondaryDelta / 2)
Truncation toward zero means ±1 secondary delta contributes 0 ("some of
the properties"). newStreak is determined by the hole outcome — identical
regardless of secondary profile.

13 Gherkin scenarios: core mechanic, amplifying pairs, dampening pairs,
zero-contribution cases, newStreak independence.

Add CLIP-based models for Challenges 3 and 4

src/clip_models.py     — CLIPBinaryClassifier + CLIPAnswerClassifier
src/clip_dataset.py    — CLIPBinaryDataset, CLIPAnswerDataset, vocab builder
src/train_clip_binary.py  — Challenge 3 training (binary, ~30 epochs, <5 min)
src/train_clip_answer.py  — Challenge 4 training (top-1000 MLP classifier)
src/predict_challenge3.py — generates submission .pkl for Challenge 3
src/predict_challenge4.py — generates submission .pkl for Challenge 4

Key design:
- L2 normalization of CLIP features before MLP
- "unanswerable" treated as a valid answer class (not filtered)
- Majority-vote answer as training target
- Closed top-1000 answer vocabulary

commit.message:
fix(storage,messaging,reconstruct): BCC redaction in inbox copies, proper BCC archival, reconstruct BCC coverage

Storage (lib.rs):
- Add redact_message_bcc_for_inbox() to strip BCC recipients from inbox
  copies while preserving them in canonical and outbox archives, ensuring
  hidden recipients stay hidden in recipient inboxes per email semantics
- Extract render_message_bundle_content() helper for consistent
  frontmatter+body formatting across canonical/outbox/inbox paths
- Only generate redacted content when BCC list is non-empty, avoiding
  unnecessary work for messages without BCC recipients
- Add comprehensive test assertions verifying canonical and outbox retain
  full BCC list while inbox copies receive redacted (empty) BCC

Messaging (messaging.rs):
- Fix send_message and reply_to_message to include resolved_bcc_recipients
  in the message bundle frontmatter instead of hardcoded empty array,
  enabling proper BCC archival in the canonical store

Reconstruct (reconstruct.rs):
- Update test fixture to include BCC recipient (Carol) for coverage
- Update assertion counts: 2 agents → 3 (Alice + Bob + Carol),
  1 recipient → 2 (to:Bob + bcc:Carol)
- Add assertions verifying recipient kinds (bcc vs to) and agent names
  are correctly reconstructed from archive

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add GPT-5-mini results on stratified cloze and generation tasks

Cloze (500 probes, stratified): 75.0% exact, 81.4% tier accuracy
Generation (200 probes, dialogue continuation): 40.5% tier accuracy, 54% avoidance

honorific tier but struggles to produce it in free generation.

fix(qbittorrent): remove postStart hook that broke VPN routing

The lifecycle hook deleted WireGuard routing policy rules (table
51820), causing traffic to leak outside the tunnel. This made
outbound port 443 traffic hit Traefik via the router's port
forwarding instead of exiting through Mullvad.

repository.description: Distributed evolutionary self-improvement agent

commit.message:
feat(elicitation): add MCP elicitation support with graceful fallback

Add server-ref and elicitation utility modules that enable tool handlers
to request user input during execution. Includes elicitSelection(),
elicitText(), and elicitConfirmation() helpers with automatic graceful
fallback when client doesn't support elicitation.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: syncro-mcp
repository.description: MCP server for Syncro MSP — tickets, assets, contacts, invoices, and RMM tools for AI assistants

commit.message:
fix: sub-task title 2-line display and click-to-edit (#123)

* fix: sub-task title 2-line display and click-to-edit (#116)

- Replace single-line truncation with CSS 2-line clamp on .subtask-title
- Add click-to-edit interaction: title toggles to input on click
- Save on Enter/blur, cancel on Escape, reject empty titles
- Show success toast on save (reuses existing showToast)
- Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, aria-label for keyboard access
- Hover/focus affordance: cursor pointer + underline

Refs #116

* fix: stabilize archive-dialog date tests against timezone drift

The daysAgoISO() helper used the current time, so the ms-level delay
between creating the test date and rendering could cause Math.floor to
round down by one day. Anchoring to noon eliminates the boundary issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
